GATEKEEPER_PORT.description: Host port the gatekeeper listens on for incoming Wyoming-satellite connections (HA Voice PE devices or Wyoming clients).

STT_GPU_PASSTHROUGH.description: Whether to request 'nvidia.com/gpu=1' on faster-whisper. Set to 'yes' if the host has a CDI-registered NVIDIA GPU; leave empty for CPU-only (then pick a smaller WHISPER_MODEL too — large-v3 is unusable on CPU for live voice).

WHISPER_MODEL.description: Whisper model. large-v3 needs GPU for natural latency (~50 ms / 3 s audio); medium and small run on CPU at degraded speed (~1-5 s / 3 s audio). Pair with STT_GPU_PASSTHROUGH.

WHISPER_LANGUAGE.description: Forced language for transcription. Auto-detect costs a few hundred ms; pin to the household language to skip it.

PIPER_VOICE.description: Piper voice model. Defaults to a German medium-quality voice; switch per harness later (Phase 4: per-family-member voice).

WAKEWORD_MODEL.description: openWakeWord model to preload. Phase 0 sticks with the bundled 'hey_jarvis'; a custom 'Oscar' model arrives in Phase 4.

HERMES_URL.description: Base URL of the host-installed Hermes Agent's HTTP API. With Hermes on the same host and pod hostNetwork that's http://127.0.0.1:<hermes-port>; otherwise the LAN IP (e.g. http://192.168.178.100:8000).

HERMES_TOKEN.description: Bearer token the gatekeeper presents when calling Hermes. Configure on the Hermes side via `hermes config set` if you want HTTP auth; leave empty if you don't.

PUSH_TOKEN.description: Bearer token Hermes presents on the gatekeeper's push endpoint (POST /push). Leave blank during local-only testing — the port isn't host-mapped — but set a value once external callers might hit it.

VOICE_PE_DEVICES.description: JSON map from short device name to its Wyoming URI, e.g. `{"office": "tcp://192.168.1.10:10700", "bedroom": "tcp://192.168.1.11:10700"}`. The push endpoint resolves `voice-pe:<name>` against this map. Leave as `{}` until you've paired at least one device.

TZ.description: IANA time zone for log timestamps.
